behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module handoff boundary: HTTP handler + signature verifier + queue adapter. A pure unit of the HMAC function would miss bugs where the handler still enqueues on auth failure. No real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process wiring is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler with an invalid `X-Signature-256` and a spy/fake queue in the same process; assert HTTP 401 and zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (handler vs persistent queue write). Unit tests of either module alone cannot catch incorrect sequencing. Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require real network/OS effects, only that write happens before ack.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process handler + real queue module (or faithful test double exposing completion point). Delay queue write completion, then assert response is not sent until write resolves; verify write precedes 200.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets orchestration boundary: enqueue/dispatch vs HTTP response lifecycle. A unit of one function could miss async wiring mistakes. Rejected System: concrete text only requires not waiting for downstream completion, which can be reproduced with in-process delayed downstream promises.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with downstream processor that blocks for a long time; assert HTTP response completes quickly while downstream task remains running.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real-time and concurrency under load ("within 3 seconds wall-clock" and "slow and concurrent"). In-process mocks/fake timers can hide event-loop starvation or server scheduling failures. This crosses into real HTTP/OS timing behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service as a real process, generate concurrent traffic with earlier slow events in flight, and measure end-to-end response latency; fail if ack latency exceeds 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at state handoff boundary (handler + dedup store/queue). Unit logic alone can miss race/order or persistence interaction defects. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess; local module wiring with persistent state is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the handler against the real dedup/queue modules in-process; assert only one persisted queue write.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: This is translation of a dependency outcome (queue full) into HTTP semantics (503 + header). Unit tests of status mapping helper alone can miss handler wiring. Rejected System: no concrete requirement for real OS/network behavior; in-process queue-full signal reproduces failure.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue module (or controlled fake) to return capacity error; invoke handler and assert HTTP 503 plus correct `Retry-After` header and no success ack.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is queue implementation + persistence layer interaction. Needs real queue/storage module behavior, not just a mocked queue. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on capacity enforcement logic, and this is reproducible with local DB/filesystem integration without real HTTP/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize queue with small configured capacity using its real persistence backend (local DB/filesystem), push events to limit, then assert next enqueue is rejected and persisted count does not exceed configured max.
